---
title: "Module 05: Advanced Models"
---

## Learning objectives

- Name **random forests**, **gradient boosting** (e.g. XGBoost), and **neural networks** as the usual “strong tabular learners” after single trees.
- Explain the **capacity vs validation** tradeoff: more flexibility needs tighter resampling, more careful tuning, and clearer metrics.
- Describe how to **swap the model spec** inside the **Tuesday / Module 04 `workflow()`** — change `parsnip` + `set_engine()`, keep recipe and folds.

## Key messages

- **Same preprocessing + cross-validation + score sheet**, different **fitting engine** underneath.
- **Never** skip the “what would break on small *n* penguins?” conversation when demoing huge models.

## Model families (quick reference)

| `parsnip` spec | Idea | Typical engine | Use when… |
|----------------|------|----------------|-----------|
| `rand_forest()` | Bagged trees + random features | `ranger` | Strong tabular default, few knobs |
| `boost_tree()` | Sequential error correction | `xgboost` | Max accuracy with tuning budget |
| `mlp()` | Single hidden layer net | `nnet` | API literacy; often weak on tiny *n* |
| `decision_tree()` | One interpretable tree | `rpart` | Teaching splits; Tuesday baseline |

Discover hyperparameters: `extract_parameter_set_dials(spec)` · [tidymodels model list](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/).

## Concept outline

### Random forests

- Bagged trees + feature subsampling; lower variance than one tree.

### Boosting (XGBoost)

- Sequential trees targeting residuals; **boosting boundary** slides on [Day 4](../slides/day-04-thursday.qmd) show surfaces sharpening round by round.

### Neural networks (tabular)

- Flexible but data-hungry — compare honestly with forests in [Module 08](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).

### Recipe extensions (Day 4)

- **`step_pca()`** — dimensionality reduction inside CV
- **`step_impute_*()`**, **`step_upsample()`** — missing data and imbalance without leakage

## Course links

- **Slides (executable):** [Day 4 — Advanced models](../slides/day-04-thursday.qmd) — includes under `_includes/day04-*.qmd`
- **Pipeline reference:** [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd)
- **Compare three engines:** [Module 08](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)
- **Metrics & SHAP:** [Module 06](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)
- **Notebook (glm + rpart, tidymodels splits):** [penguins-classification.Rmd](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd)
- [Module library](index.qmd)
